en.extract:
The pale-blue monarch (Hypothymis puella) is a small passerine bird in the family Monarchidae endemic to eastern Indonesia.


Taxonomy and systematics
The pale-blue monarch was originally described in the genus Myiagra and then classified as subspecies of the widespread black-naped monarch, Hypothymis azurea, until split by the IOC in 2013 to describe a new species. Some other authourities have not yet adopted this species split. The alternate name Moluccan monarch should not be confused with the species of the same name, Symposiachrus bimaculatus. Additional alternate names for the pale-blue monarch include Pacific monarch (a name shared with the buff-bellied monarch), Pacific small monarch and small monarch.


Subspecies
Two subspecies are recognized:

H. p. puella - (Wallace, 1863): Found on Sulawesi and adjacent islands
H. p. blasii - Hartert, 1898: Found in the Banggai archipelago.


Behaviour and ecology
The pale-blue monarch is a forest species which builds a small cup nest. It has short legs and sits very upright whilst perched prominently, like a shrike. It is insectivorous, often hunting by flycatching.

The '''pale-blue monarch''' ('''''Hypothymis puella''''') is a small [[passerine]] [[bird]] in the family [[Monarchidae]] [[Endemism|endemic]] to eastern Indonesia.

==Taxonomy and systematics==
The pale-blue monarch was originally described in the genus ''[[Myiagra]]'' and then classified as [[subspecies]] of the widespread [[black-naped monarch]], ''Hypothymis azurea'', until split by the IOC in 2013 to describe a new species.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/archive-3-1-to-3-5/species-3-1-3-5/|title=Species 3.1-3.5 « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|access-date=2017-01-01}}</ref> Some other authourities have not yet adopted this species split.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?lang=EN&avibaseid=11B2988D&sec=summary&ssver=1|title=Hypothymis puella blasii - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2016-10-22}}</ref> The alternate name '''Moluccan monarch''' should not be confused with the species of the same name, ''[[Symposiachrus bimaculatus]]''. Additional alternate names for the pale-blue monarch include '''Pacific monarch''' (a name shared with the [[buff-bellied monarch]]), '''Pacific small monarch''' and '''small monarch'''.
[[File:HypothymispuellaBR.jpg|left|thumb|Pale-blue monarch (Hypothymis puella) from Tangkoko, Sulawesi]]

===Subspecies===
Two subspecies are recognized:<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|title=IOC World Bird List 6.3|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|journal=IOC World Bird List Datasets|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.6.3|doi-access=free}}</ref>
* ''H. p. puella'' - <small>([[Alfred Russel Wallace|Wallace]], 1863)</small>: Found on [[Sulawesi]] and adjacent islands
* ''H. p. blasii'' - <small>[[Ernst Hartert|Hartert]], 1898</small>: Found in the [[Banggai regency|Banggai]] archipelago.

==Behaviour and ecology==
The pale-blue monarch is a forest species which builds a small cup nest. It has short legs and sits very upright whilst perched prominently, like a [[shrike]]. It is [[insectivorous]], often hunting by flycatching.
